title: Programming Paradigms
summary: 9618 P4 — imperative, object-oriented, and declarative paradigms.
learningObjectives: Match paradigm to suitable problem types.

simpleExplanation.title: Different ways to program
simpleExplanation.summary: A paradigm is an approach to structuring programs. Cambridge 9618 focuses on imperative/OOP vs declarative (e.g. SQL, Prolog concepts).
steps: OOP: objects with data and methods.
Same problem can suit different paradigms.

sections.content: **Programming paradigms** (20.1) links **11.x programming** to **P4 design**. Give **concrete language examples** where asked.
